note: Seed file with hand-written sample questions. Will be replaced by scraped data in questions.json.

questions.question: A flexible balloon at the surface has a volume of 12 litres. What is its volume at 30 metres in seawater (assume 1 ATA per 10 m)?
questions.explanation: At 30 m the absolute pressure is 4 ATA. Boyle's Law: V2 = V1 × P1/P2 = 12 × 1/4 = 3 litres.

questions.question: Nitrogen narcosis is generally first noticeable at depths of approximately:
questions.explanation: Symptoms of nitrogen narcosis usually become noticeable around 30 m / 100 ft and increase with depth, though individual susceptibility varies.

questions.question: At 40 m / 132 ft (5 ATA), the partial pressure of oxygen in air (21% O2) is approximately:
questions.explanation: PO2 = fO2 × ATA = 0.21 × 5 = 1.05 ATA. Above 1.4 ATA the risk of CNS oxygen toxicity rises significantly.

questions.question: When assisting an SSI Open Water Instructor with confined water sessions, the maximum student-to-instructor ratio (with one certified assistant) is typically:
questions.explanation: Per SSI Training Standards, OW confined water max ratio is 8:1, extendable by +2 per certified assistant. (Verify against current SSI Training Standards before exam.)

questions.question: Which factor is NOT typically considered when planning a guided dive for certified divers?

options.d: Personal preference of the dive guide regardless of group

questions.explanation: Dive planning must always consider environmental conditions and the least experienced diver. The guide's personal preference does not override group safety.

questions.question: When demonstrating a skill to students, the demonstration should be performed:

options.a: As fast as possible to save time
options.b: At normal speed with poor technique to make it relatable
options.c: Slowly and exaggerated, with perfect technique
options.d: Only verbally — students learn better from explanation

questions.explanation: Skills should be demonstrated slowly and exaggerated with perfect technique so students can clearly observe each step and replicate it correctly.

questions.question: The maximum depth for SSI Open Water Diver training dives is:
questions.explanation: Per SSI standards, OW Diver training dives are limited to 18 m / 60 ft maximum depth. (Always verify current SSI Training Standards.)

questions.question: SSI's teaching methodology often referenced as 'EDGE' stands for:
questions.explanation: EDGE = Explain, Demonstrate, Guide, Evaluate — SSI's instructional model for teaching skills.
